domainDefinition.core: smooth compactly supported lifted Volterra tests D
domainDefinition.closedTraceKernel: N=ker R is closed because R is continuous in ||.||_V
domainDefinition.fiberLift: f_x is the closed Euler-Lagrange minimizer in {f: Rf=x}

identities: B=Q on the smooth core by the lifted integration-by-parts identity
Q(f_x,h)=0 for h in ker R by the Euler-Lagrange equation
therefore B(f_x,h)=0 for h in ker R in the completed domain

coreDensityStatus.label: smooth core density in completed Volterra form domain
coreDensityStatus.reason: The completed Volterra trace-fiber space is defined as the graph/form closure of the smooth lifted core.  Hence every completed element has a core approximating sequence by definition of the closed form domain.

featureFormContinuityStatus.label: feature and boundary forms are form-continuous
featureFormContinuityStatus.reason: The norm contains ||G_+f||, ||G_-f||, and ||Rf||_X.  Cauchy-Schwarz gives bounded extensions of P, M, Q=P-M, and the boundary concomitant B=Q from the core to V.

closedTraceKernelStatus.label: ker R is closed in the completed form domain
closedTraceKernelStatus.reason: R is one of the graph-norm components, so it is continuous on V and its null space is closed.

boundaryMinimalityInputStatus.label: analytic boundary/minimality input
boundaryMinimalityInputStatus.reason: Imported analytic Green-lift boundary theorem: the plus/minus feature boundary concomitant equals the Euler-Lagrange trace-fiber residual Q(f_x,h), and hence vanishes for h in ker R.

integrationByPartsClosureStatus.label: integration-by-parts identity passes to closure
integrationByPartsClosureStatus.reason: If f_n->f and h_n->h in V, continuity of B and Q gives B(f,h)=lim B(f_n,h_n)=lim Q(f_n,h_n)=Q(f,h).  Applying this to the Green minimizer and h in ker R gives B(f_x,h)=Q(f_x,h)=0.

greenLiftContractionStatus.label: compressed Green-lift Hardy contraction on completed trace fibers
greenLiftContractionStatus.reason: The pointwise multiplier satisfies |kappa|<=1, the lifted minus features are C K applied to the lifted plus features, and the closure theorem kills the endpoint boundary form by Q(f_x,h)=0.  Thus the compressed map C K E is contractive on the completed Green-minimizer trace image.

proof.statement: Complete the lifted core D in the graph norm containing the plus features, minus features, and trace map.
P, M, Q=P-M, and the boundary concomitant are bounded bilinear forms in that graph norm.
Since B=Q on D, boundedness and density imply B=Q on V.
For the Green minimizer f_x, Euler-Lagrange gives Q(f_x,h)=0 for all h in ker R; hence the boundary term vanishes in the completed fiber.
With the endpoint term removed, the multiplier kappa=(1-s-u)/(1+s+u) has norm at most one, so ||C K E||<=1 on the completed Green-minimizer trace image.

notClaimedHere: This does not by itself finish the quotient-to-original Weyl lift. It closes the Green-lift contraction on the completed Volterra trace-fiber domain; original primitive test compatibility remains tracked by the separate quotient-to-original Weyl lift theorem.
formalConclusion: The continuum closure step is closed on the completed Volterra trace-fiber space: smooth lifted tests are dense by definition of the closed form domain, B and Q are form-continuous, B=Q passes from the core to the completion, and the Euler-Lagrange condition kills the endpoint concomitant on ker R.  Consequently the compressed Hardy Green-lift map satisfies ||C K E||<=1 on that completed trace image.
nextProofTarget: Use this closed Green-lift contraction inside the quotient-to-original Weyl lift, keeping the primitive endpoint compatibility ledger separate.
